info.title: ZeroSettle IAP API
info.description: The ZeroSettle IAP API powers the iOS, Android, and Flutter SDKs. It enables product catalog fetching, web checkout, entitlement management, subscription lifecycle operations, and StoreKit transaction syncing. All endpoints are authenticated via the `X-ZeroSettle-Key` header.

tags.name: Products
tags.description: Fetch the product catalog for your app

tags.name: Checkout
tags.description: Create checkout sessions and payment intents for web purchases

tags.name: Entitlements
tags.description: Query user entitlements (active purchases and subscriptions)

tags.name: Cancel Flow
tags.description: Configurable cancel flow with retention offers

get.description: Fetches the product catalog for your app, including web and StoreKit prices, checkout configuration, trial eligibility, and any active migration campaign. Pass `user_id` to get per-user trial eligibility.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: The user's external ID. When provided, the response includes per-user `is_trial_eligible` on each product.

migration_campaign.description: Active Switch & Save migration campaign, or null if none is configured.

post.description: Creates a Stripe PaymentIntent for native checkout (Apple Pay / Google Pay payment sheet). Returns a `client_secret` for confirming the payment on the client side and a `checkout_url` for fallback browser checkout.

external_user_id.description: The user's external ID. Either this or `user_id` is required.

user_id.description: Alias for `external_user_id`. Either this or `external_user_id` is required.

platform.description: The platform the purchase is being made from.

stripe_customer_id.description: Existing Stripe customer ID to attach the payment to.

checkout_domain.description: Custom domain for the checkout page URL.

checkout_mode.description: Preferred checkout mode. `native` uses the in-app payment sheet; `browser` opens a web checkout page.

storekit_subscription_end.description: ISO 8601 datetime of the current StoreKit subscription's expiry. Used for Switch & Save migrations to align the trial period.

storekit_original_transaction_id.description: The StoreKit original transaction ID for the subscription being migrated.

post.description: Creates Stripe PaymentIntents for multiple products in a single request. Reduces N sequential requests to 1, with shared Stripe customer/account resolution. Products that fail validation return an error entry instead of failing the entire batch. Maximum 25 products per request.

products.description: Array of products to create payment intents for. Maximum 25 items.

storekit_subscription_end.description: ISO 8601 datetime of the current StoreKit subscription's expiry. Used for Switch & Save migrations.

storekit_original_transaction_id.description: The StoreKit original transaction ID for the subscription being migrated.

platform.description: The platform the purchase is being made from.

stripe_customer_id.description: Existing Stripe customer ID to attach payments to.

200.description: Batch results with per-product success or error

results.description: Array of results, one per requested product. Each entry is either a successful PaymentIntent response or an error object.

client_secret.description: Stripe PaymentIntent client secret. Present on success.

transaction_id.description: ZeroSettle transaction ID. Present on success.

amount.description: Charge amount in cents. Present on success.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code. Present on success.

product_name.description: Display name of the product. Present on success.

original_amount.description: Full price before discounts/trials. Present on success.

checkout_url.description: Fallback URL for browser-based checkout. Present on success.

publishable_key.description: Stripe publishable key for confirming the payment. Present on success.

stripe_account.description: Connected Stripe account ID for BYOS. Null for managed mode.

merchant_country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code for Apple Pay.

is_subscription.description: Whether this product is a subscription. Present on success.

subscription_interval.description: Subscription billing interval (e.g., `month`, `week`). Present for subscriptions.

trial_type.description: Type of trial applied. Omitted when no trial.

trial_end.description: Unix timestamp when trial ends. Only present with active trial.

pending_amount.description: Amount billed when trial ends. Only present with active trial.

error.description: Error message. Present when this product failed validation.

post.description: Creates a Stripe Checkout Session for browser-based checkout. Returns a URL to redirect the user to Stripe's hosted checkout page.

external_user_id.description: The user's external ID. Either this or `user_id` is required.

user_id.description: Alias for `external_user_id`. Either this or `external_user_id` is required.

platform.description: The platform the purchase is being made from.

stripe_customer_id.description: Existing Stripe customer ID to attach the payment to.

rc_app_user_id.description: RevenueCat app user ID, if using RevenueCat integration.

get.description: Retrieves the status and details of a specific transaction by its ID.

parameters.name: transaction_id
parameters.description: The transaction ID (UUID returned from checkout).

patch.description: Updates the StoreKit finish status for a transaction. Called by the SDK after `transaction.finish()` succeeds (status `1`) or if finishing is pending (status `2`).

get.description: Returns a paginated list of transactions for a given user, ordered by most recent first.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of transactions to return. Default: 50, Max: 100.

get.description: Returns all entitlements for a user, including active subscriptions, one-time purchases, and expired items. Use this to determine what the user has access to.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: The user's external ID. Required unless `email` is provided.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: The user's email address. Deprecated -- use `user_id` instead.

post.description: Forwards a StoreKit 2 JWS (JSON Web Signature) transaction to ZeroSettle for verification and recording. The backend verifies the JWS against Apple's root certificate chain, creates a `Transaction` and `Entitlement`, and returns success. The SDK should call `transaction.finish()` only after receiving a success response.

jws_representation.description: The StoreKit 2 transaction's JWS representation string.

get.description: Returns the current status of a StoreKit subscription by its original transaction ID. Useful for checking renewal state and expiry.

parameters.name: original_transaction_id
parameters.description: The StoreKit original transaction ID for the subscription.

post.description: Reports that a Switch & Save migration was shown or converted. Used to track migration campaign analytics.

variant_id.description: The experiment variant ID, if the migration campaign is part of an A/B test.

conversions.description: Total number of conversions for this campaign.

post.description: Cancels a user's active web checkout subscription. By default, access continues until the end of the current billing period (`immediate: false`). Set `immediate: true` to revoke access immediately.

product_id.description: Specific product to cancel. If omitted, cancels the user's active subscription.

immediate.description: If `true`, cancels immediately and revokes access. If `false`, cancels at end of billing period.

post.description: Pauses a user's active web checkout subscription. The subscription will automatically resume after the pause duration expires.

product_id.description: Specific product to pause. If omitted, pauses the user's active subscription.

pause_duration_days.description: Number of days to pause the subscription. If omitted, uses the default configured in the cancel flow.

post.description: Resumes a previously paused web checkout subscription.

product_id.description: Specific product to resume. If omitted, resumes the user's paused subscription.

post.description: Creates a Stripe Customer Portal session URL. The portal lets users manage their subscriptions, update payment methods, and view billing history.

return_url.description: URL to redirect the user to after they leave the portal.

portal_url.description: URL to redirect the user to the Stripe Customer Portal.

get.description: Returns the configured cancel flow for the app, including survey questions, retention offers, and pause options. The SDK uses this to render a multi-step cancellation experience.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: The user's external ID. Used to personalize offers.

post.description: Submits the user's responses from the cancel flow, including survey answers and the final outcome (cancelled, retained, dismissed, or paused). Used for cancel flow analytics.

outcome.description: The final outcome of the cancel flow.

offer_shown.description: Whether a retention offer was shown to the user.

offer_accepted.description: Whether the user accepted the retention offer.

pause_shown.description: Whether a pause option was shown.

pause_accepted.description: Whether the user chose to pause.

pause_duration_days.description: Number of days the user chose to pause, if applicable.

last_step_seen.description: The last step of the cancel flow the user saw.

variant_id.description: Experiment variant ID, if the cancel flow is part of an A/B test.

post.description: Accepts the retention offer shown during the cancel flow, applying a discount to the user's subscription.

product_id.description: The product the offer applies to.

discount_applied.description: Whether the discount was successfully applied.

get.description: Checks if a user is eligible for a subscription upgrade and returns available upgrade options.

parameters.name: product_id
parameters.description: Specific product to check upgrade eligibility for.

post.description: Executes a subscription upgrade, creating a new payment intent or checkout session for the target product. The old subscription is cancelled with proration when the new payment succeeds.

current_product_id.description: The reference ID of the user's current subscription product.

target_product_id.description: The reference ID of the product to upgrade to.

post.description: Records the user's response to an upgrade offer for analytics purposes.

accepted.description: Whether the user accepted the upgrade offer.

variant_id.description: Experiment variant ID, if the upgrade offer is part of an A/B test.

post.description: Tracks an analytics event from the SDK. Used for funnel analysis, screen views, and custom events.

product_id.description: The product related to this event, if applicable.

screen_name.description: The screen or view where the event occurred.

metadata.description: Additional key-value metadata for the event.

post.description: Restores purchases for a user by linking entitlements from a previous identity (matched by email) to the current user ID. Useful when a user reinstalls the app or signs in on a new device.

email.description: The user's email address to match against previous purchases.

user_id.description: The user's current external ID to restore purchases to.

ApiKeyAuth.name: X-ZeroSettle-Key
ApiKeyAuth.description: Your publishable API key. Use `zs_pk_test_*` for sandbox or `zs_pk_live_*` for production.

web_price.description: The web checkout price, or null if not available for web purchase.

storekit_price.description: The StoreKit price from App Store Connect, or null if not synced.

subscription_group_id.description: The subscription group this product belongs to, or null for non-subscription products.

free_trial_duration.description: ISO 8601 duration for the free trial period (e.g., `P7D` for 7 days), or null if no trial is configured.

is_trial_eligible.description: Whether the current user is eligible for a free trial on this product. Null if `user_id` was not provided in the request.

Price.description: A price in micros (1/1,000,000 of the currency unit).

CheckoutConfig.description: Configuration for how the SDK should present checkout.

sheet_type.description: Default checkout presentation: `native` for Apple Pay / Google Pay payment sheet, `webview` for embedded web checkout.

is_enabled.description: Whether web checkout is enabled for this app.

apple_merchant_id.description: Apple Merchant ID for Apple Pay, if configured.

JurisdictionConfig.description: Checkout configuration for a specific jurisdiction.

sheet_type.description: Checkout presentation type for this jurisdiction.

is_enabled.description: Whether web checkout is enabled in this jurisdiction.

MigrationCampaign.description: An active Switch & Save migration campaign.

should_show.description: Whether the migration offer should be displayed to the user.

product_id.description: The product the migration offer applies to.

min_subscription_days.description: Minimum number of days the user must have been subscribed to be eligible.

max_subscription_days.description: Maximum number of subscription days for eligibility.

free_trial_days.description: Number of free trial days offered in the migration.

title.description: Title text for the migration offer UI.

message.description: Body text for the migration offer UI.

variant_id.description: Experiment variant ID if the campaign is part of an A/B test.

PaymentIntentResponse.description: Response from creating a payment intent.

client_secret.description: Stripe PaymentIntent client secret. Use this to confirm the payment on the client side.

transaction_id.description: ZeroSettle transaction ID. Use this to poll for transaction status.

product_name.description: Display name of the product being purchased.

merchant_country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of the merchant's Stripe account. Required for Apple Pay `paymentRequest()` country parameter.

original_amount_cents.description: The original price before any discounts or prorations.

trial_type.description: The type of trial applied to this checkout. `free_trial` for standard free trials, `migration` for Switch & Save aligned trials. Omitted when no trial is active.

trial_end.description: Unix timestamp when the trial period ends. Only present when a trial is active.

pending_amount.description: The charge amount in cents that will be billed when the trial ends. Only present when a trial is active.

CheckoutSessionResponse.description: Response from creating a checkout session.

url.description: URL to redirect the user to for checkout.

purchased_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the purchase was made.

storekit_status.description: StoreKit finish status: `1` = finished, `2` = pending. Null for web checkout transactions.

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 expiry timestamp for subscription transactions. Null for one-time purchases.

TransactionHistoryItem.description: A transaction in the user's history.

Entitlement.description: A user's entitlement to a product.

is_active.description: Whether the user currently has access to this product.

expires_at.description: When the entitlement expires, or null for lifetime purchases.

purchased_at.description: When the entitlement was originally purchased.

will_renew.description: Whether the subscription will auto-renew at the end of the current period.

is_trial.description: Whether the entitlement is currently in a free trial period.

trial_ends_at.description: When the trial period ends, or null if not on trial.

cancelled_at.description: When the subscription was cancelled, or null if not cancelled.

expires_at.description: When the subscription expires or renews.

access_until.description: When the user will lose access (end of billing period for non-immediate cancellations).

resumes_at.description: When the subscription will automatically resume.

enabled.description: Whether the cancel flow is enabled for this app.

questions.description: Survey questions to show during the cancel flow.

offer.description: Retention offer to show, or null if none is configured.

pause.description: Pause option configuration, or null if pause is not enabled.

variant_id.description: Experiment variant ID if the cancel flow is part of an A/B test.

CancelFlowQuestion.description: A survey question in the cancel flow.

CancelFlowOffer.description: A retention offer in the cancel flow.

duration_months.description: How many billing cycles the discount lasts.

CancelFlowPause.description: Pause option in the cancel flow.

enabled.description: Whether the pause option is available.

eligible.description: Whether the user is eligible for an upgrade.

current_product_id.description: The user's current subscription product ID.

eligible_upgrades.description: Products the user can upgrade to.

product_id.description: The upgrade target product's reference ID.

savings_percent.description: Percentage savings compared to the current plan (annualized).

variant_id.description: Experiment variant ID if the upgrade offer is part of an A/B test.

type.description: The type of upgrade being performed.

client_secret.description: Stripe PaymentIntent client secret for confirming the upgrade payment.

transaction_id.description: ZeroSettle transaction ID for the upgrade.

BadRequest.description: Invalid request -- missing or malformed parameters.
